observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC - Municipal Accountant provide the necessary adjusting journal
entries to reclassify the identified semi-expendable properties in
accordance with the Pro-forma entries as illustrated in COA Circular No.
2024-006 dated March 14, 2024; and

b. The OIC - Municipal Accountant ensure proper recording of semi-
expendable properties and PPE items, strictly observe the prescribed
threshold amount, and properly disclose in the Notes to the Financial
Statements (FS) all details related to semi-expendable items.

2.12 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC - Municipal Accountant provide the necessary adjusting
records to reclassify the completed projects and provide appropriate
depreciation charges;

b. The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accounting Office with a
quarterly copy of completed projects to update records;

c. The OIC - Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to FS any
movements of property, plant, and equipment to ensure transparency
and fairness in the presentation of the FS; and

d. The OIC - Municipal Accountant include in the closing instructions
before closing the books at year-end, all the necessary procedures to
be undertaken by the different offices to ensure complete and accurate
financial reports at each reporting date.

3.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC- Municipal
Accountant reconcile the Trust–Liability DRRM account and prepare the
corresponding adjusting journal entries by updating the General Ledger /
Subsidiary Ledger for the transactions related to Trust–Liability DRRM and
record transactions pertaining to the utilization and fund transfers for all
unexpended/unobligated balance made during CY 2022-2023 of the QRF
and the DRRMF-MOOE to reflect the correct amount in the Statement of
Financial Position.

4.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC – Municipal
Accountant:

a. Prepare an adjusting journal entry for the transfer of equipment to the
General Fund;

b. Record transactions in the Trust Fund books as prescribed in the IPSAS
1 and New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local
Government Units; and

a. For future fund transfers, make representation to the Source Agency
(SA) to incorporate in the Memorandum of Agreement the pro-forma
journal entries under the books of accounts of the LGU and the SA as a
guide in the correct recording of transactions.

5.8 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The Municipal Treasurer report the actual collections on the month it
was received in the Report of Collections and Deposits and provide
copies of cash count sheets to the Municipal Accounting Office
especially at year-end to ensure the accuracy and completeness of cash
in treasury account in the SFP;

b. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to
adjust the understatement of cash-local treasury amounting to
₱764,257.80 and the revenue accounts for the same amount as of
December 31, 2024;

c. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain a subsidiary
ledger for the cash–local treasury account per accountable officer,
review the breakdown of the non-moving balance, and provide
adjusting journal entries to record the receivable amount from the
former disbursing officer; and

d. Looking forward, provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to FS to
better assist users of the financial statements in understanding its
contents.

6.9 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC - Municipal Accountant conduct immediate and proper validation
of real properties based on the inventory report of the Assessor’s Office
and establish actual ownership of the land, and that future economic
benefits will flow to the Municipality in line with IPSAS 17;

b. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries
to record all lands owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section
111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector
Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1; and

c. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain a Real Property
Ledger Cards with complete details as to its description, location,
acquisition cost, additions and disposition that will serve as a subsidiary
ledger to support the balances reported in the SFP and cross balance with
the report of the assessor’s office.

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC- Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer hasten the
reconciliation of Cash in Bank records to bring the account to its correct
amount;

b. The OIC - Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant have a
uniform application of the standards in recognizing and recording cash
transactions; and

c. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entry to close
the Bank Account for DBP-GCC Bank Account after obtaining the
Certificate of Fund Transfer.

8.6 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the Monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements for DBP Bank Accounts of the Municipality
for calendar year 2024; and

b. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare necessary adjusting journal
entries to correct the deficiencies and ensure the accuracy of the
reported cash balances per fund in the Financial Statements.

9.10 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare a schedule of aging of
receivables which will become the basis for availing of this one-time
cleansing opportunity and accordingly include as additional disclosure
in the Notes to the Financial Statements;

b. A task force will be created to assist the OIC - Municipal Accountant and
visit the provisions of Items 6.0 and 7.0 of COA Circular 2023-008 for the
disposition of dormant accounts; and

c. Looking forward, ensure that receivables are properly monitored to
ensure their prompt collection and accurate recording in the FS
including the provision of an allowance for impairment on receivables
with doubtful collectability.

10.12 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC - Municipal Accountant retrace all entries made in the
reciprocal accounts and effect adjusting journal entries to correct the
previous entries made;

b. The Municipal Accounting Office maintain subsidiary ledgers with a
detailed description to determine the nature of each transaction on the
reciprocal accounts; and

c. The OIC - Municipal Accountant conduct periodic reconciliations to
ensure the fairness of records and presentation of FS.

11.25 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The GSO prepare a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)
supported with the corresponding Requisitions and Issued Slips (RIS)
to account for the issuances of office supplies, and as basis of the
MAO to prepare the JEV to record the supplies issued during the
period;

b. The requisitioning units/ concerned offices issued with inventories to
maintain a stock cards per stock number and prepare an inventory
report every semester to serve as reference for the GSO during their
conduct of actual physical count of inventories at each reporting date;

c. The Municipal Accounting Office and the GSO maintain the Supply
Ledger Cards to reconcile with the Stock Cards, respectively, to
provide a system of check and balance in the recording of inventories
in accordance with the perpetual inventory system, in keeping with the
accounting policies prescribed in the pertinent provisions of the
NGAS Manual, Volume I;

d. The GSO submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)
to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 124 of NGAS and
reconcile the reported inventories in the FS and the physical
inventories on hand including the non-moving items; after which,
prepare adjusting journal entries, if warranted; and

e. Provide adequate disclosures as prescribed under Paragraph 47 of
IPSAS 12 to enhance understanding of the users of the financial
statements.

12.13 We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting journal entries
and reclassify the nine (9) deceased horses from Breeding Stocks to
Work/Zoo Animals and appropriately compute for the depreciation
expenses;

b. The GSO, Municipal Agriculturist and the OIC - Municipal Accountant
benchmark from other Municipalities regarding the determination of
fair value of biological assets, revisit the provisions of IPSAS 27
regarding recognition and measurement and adequate disclosures to
fairly present the biological assets in the SFP.

c. The GSO and the Municipal Agriculturist determine the persons in
charge of the deceased animals, immediately request relief from
accountability, and impose appropriate sanctions, if warranted; and

d. Looking forward, incorporate in the Memorandum of Agreement the
processes to be undertaken in case of loss or death and the penalty
clauses in case of non-performance of responsibilities and
immediately furnish the Accounting Office for their reference in
properly recording the said procurement of animals.

13.9 We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC – Municipal
Accountant:

a. Prepare adjusting journal entries to rectify the misclassifications of
financial liabilities and ensure proper review of the recorded
transactions per account in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular
No. 2015-09 or the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs dated
December 1, 2015;

b. Prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledger (SL) for Accounts Payable
and aging of the Accounts Payable determined to be under the Unpaid
Obligation pertaining to the prior year; and

c. Make a coordinated effort amongst the offices in the Municipality and
review the charges to accounts payable, after which derecognize
those that are not valid claims to ensure that only those valid financial
liabilities are recognized as Account Payable.

14.9 We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC - Municipal
Accountant:

a. Ensure proper review of the FS submitted to be free from material
misstatements so that its readers and users have a better grasp of the
Municipality’s operations; and

b. Provide all line items with adequate disclosure in compliance with
Paragraph 127 (c) of IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2016-004, to
provide additional information and value for a better understanding of
accounting data as presented in the Financial Statements at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:
The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the Cash in Bank Account; and
Regularly Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement in order to present reliable Cash in Bank balance.

We recommended that for PPE items with an acquisition cost of P50,000.00 and below, direct the Municipal Accountant to make the appropriate adjusting entries to reclassify the affected PPE in accordance with COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022, with due consideration of the issuance of the Inventory Custodianship Slip to establish accountability of the end-user.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Review the Due to NGAs account, and submit liquidation reports on its completed projects to the source agency to remove it from the records;
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to return the excess funds; and
Establish separate subsidiary ledgers for fund transfers to facilitate better monitoring and reporting of fund utilizations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to provide adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recognition of fund transfer; and
Require the presence of a MOA for fund transfers.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office to continue the preparation and maintenance of a detailed ledger card for each class of PPE in soft copies, review the composition and balances of each PPE item to reconcile the noted variance of ₱5,078,726.07 between the balances per supporting schedule and as presented in the Statement of Financial Position (SFP) to comply with Section 114(2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office in coordination with the members of the Inventory Committee to fast-track the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and ensure the immediate and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the Municipality’s PPEs.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide additional disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2023 the required details/information for the Property, Plant and Equipment including Total Road Network System in accordance with IPSAS provisions and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant, GSO Designate, and the Municipal Assessor to evaluate their records so that the necessary adjusting entries shall be made to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and 17 for a fair presentation of the land account in the Statement of Financial Position at year-end.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a schedule of aging of receivables which will become the basis for availing of this one-time cleansing opportunity and accordingly include as additional disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements;
Create a task force to assist the Municipal Accountant and visit the provisions of Items 6.0 and 7.0 of COA Circular 2023-008 for the disposition of dormant accounts; and
Looking forward, ensure that receivables are properly monitored to ensure their prompt collection and accurate recording in the FS including the provision of an allowance for impairment on receivables with doubtful collectability.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Agriculturist to request assistance from the Department of Agriculture in determining the fair value of biological assets at every reporting period;
Require the GSO and the Municipal Agriculturist to determine the persons in charge of the deceased animals and impose appropriate sanctions, if warranted; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures under the provisions of IPSAS 27 on the fair presentation of biological assets.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation charges to the PPEs enumerated above and provide a copy of the journal entry vouchers together with the detailed computation in taking up depreciation charges to the Auditor for verification;
Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to coordinate with the General Services Office in the updating of PPE accounts together with its corresponding property ledger cards; and
Thoroughly investigate the root cause(s) of the noted abnormal balance of the Other PPE account and provide adjusting journal entries with detailed computation to ensure fair presentation of the PPE balances.
Looking forward, submit a lapsing schedule showing the computation of depreciation expenses together with journal entry vouchers.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Continue the review of outstanding checks and coordinate with the Municipal Treasury that they will be furnished with the list of checks staled and canceled or checks staled but reissued together with the original disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents to facilitate accurate preparation of journal entry vouchers and ensure proper recording of the same.
Submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for October to December 2023 following COA Circular No. 96-011 for prompt verification.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Office of the Mayor, in coordination with the BAC to revisit the terms of the contract agreement with the supplier that will align the purpose of the MOA and direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adjusting journal entries to recognize the deliveries made by the supplier at year-end totaling ₱1,653,774.50; and
Strictly adhere to the principles of accrual accounting by recognizing expenses when incurred, and revenues earned when services are rendered.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to answer the letter above-mentioned, submit the lacking documents if available, and provide a valid justification for the deficiencies noted for reference in the audit; and
Direct the BAC to thoroughly examine each prospective bidder’s submitted bid documents, especially first-time suppliers/ contractors to enter into joint venture agreements before awarding contracts in compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 and GPPB NPM.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the HRMO in cooperation with the Internal Audit Services in establishing policy(ies) that strictly enforce the minimum take-home pay of   ₱5,000.00; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to double–check before signing and certifying the pay slips of employees wishing to avail of loan packages that said employee’s minimum take-home pay is well within the required amount in compliance with Section 56 of RA 11936.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the timely submission of disbursement vouchers for the grant of cash advance for CF with complete supporting documents as prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular 2015-01.

We recommended that Management:
Include an item of expenditure for foreign travel in the preparation of annual budget to ensure that future foreign travels have an appropriate funding source;
Immediately submit the liquidation report of the foreign travel to Australia, for our reference in audit; and
Looking forward, strictly adhere to the provisions on budgeting and principles of disbursements regarding the payment of expenditures.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the necessary subsidiary records as required under Section 114(2) of PD 1445, provide the details of the Accounts Payable (source documents, payee, nature of liability), and submit written justification on the noted deficiencies on supporting documents in the recognition of accounts payable to complete the evaluation and to enable us to decide on audit; and
Determine whether the said payable is still due and demandable. Otherwise, revert the balance of accounts payable to the unappropriated general fund according to the provisions of PD 1445 to fairly present the liability accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Require the members of the Local School Board to be prompt in their submission of PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat for its review and consolidation into the proposed APP and see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for public bidding to enhance compliance with the requirement of law regarding procurement.
Direct the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 in the proper procurement of supplies and materials; and
Stop the practice of splitting purchases to promote transparency and fairness in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Management refrain from utilizing the LDRRMF for non-disaster related programs, projects, and/or activities in conformity with Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and Section 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 to ensure proper fund utilization, accurate reporting and attain the purpose for which the fund was intended.

We recommended to the Management that:
Looking forward, strengthen the internal controls placed in the procurement of medicines by directing the MHO to coordinate with the GSO to take into consideration the latest updates on the drug price references index during the preparation of Purchase Requests; and
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to procure drugs and medicines at reasonable prices guided by the applicable DPRI set by DOH in accordance with DOH Administrative Order No. 2019-0040 dated October 14, 2019, to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
The Inventory Committee assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.
For CY 2022, the Inventory Committee prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.
Each office of the LGU fill up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper.
Moreover, the Inventory Committee shall record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.
The Property Unit prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.
The LCE ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Management:
Revisit the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 to revitalize the agency on the accounting and reporting guidelines of local roads;
Direct the Accounting, Engineering, and GSO offices to coordinate with each other as regards the recording and reporting of local roads to ensure that local roads are properly tagged with their respective road network IDs and other technical details are properly noted; and finally
Direct the Municipal Accountant to include the details of local roads in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the Management stop the practice of providing depreciation charges on the following year for properties recently acquired on the current year. Instead, direct the Municipal Accounting Office to religiously observe the provisions of IPSAS 17 by providing the appropriate depreciation charges when the PPEs are available for use in order to properly reflect their respective carrying amounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
Expedite the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements by trying other alternative measures like securing snapshots and/or register in the LBP we-access application if the bank statements are not available so that the submission of the BRS will not be impeded.
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile the Cash in Bank account balances with the Treasurer’s cashbooks by determining the transactions that caused the net difference of                    ₱ (298,827.15).
Thereafter, conduct quarterly reconciliation of such balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.
We recommended that the Management reconcile the differing balances in the reciprocal accounts in order to ensure that balances as reported in the financial statements are fairly presented.

We recommended that the Management adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 27 - Agriculture by:
Maintaining updated records on biological assets acquired through purchase or donation by coordinating with the General Services Office and Municipal Agriculturist’s Office;
Recognition of gains or losses in changes of fair market value less costs to sell to be reflected in the surplus or deficit for the period on which it arises; and
Including the breeding stocks/biological assets during the periodic conduct of physical count of the municipality’s properties.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the rules and regulations on procurement for scientific, scholarly or artistic work, exclusive technology and media services as provided under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and on the grant of cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification/s on granting cash advance other than traveling expenses to an elected official; and
Immediately submit the corresponding liquidation reports and stop granting cash advance for elected officials other than for official travel.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit a justification/s on granting cash retirement assistance to its employees and its inclusion to the Collection Negotiation Agreement of Municipality of Las Nieves Employees Association contrary to the above cited provisions; and
Stop the practice of granting monetary retirement awards without legal basis and remove such clause in the Collection Negotiation Agreement.

We recommended that the Management direct the MSWDO in coordination with the Office of the Mayor to expedite the completion of the crafted policy on the payment/grant of AICS in line with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02 series of 2014 as amended by Memorandum Circular No. 15 series of 2014 to set forth the guidelines in strengthening and enhancing the implementation of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations (AICS) of the Municipality to ensure proper reporting, auditing and monitoring and uphold the delivery of services to its constituents.

We recommended that the Management:
Immediately stop the present system of handling petty cash fund and ensure compliance with the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units in maintaining the PCF under the imprest system;
Make the necessary adjustments on the entries made; and
Require that disbursements from the PC shall be made through the Petty Cash Voucher (PCV).

Relative thereto, we recommended the following improvement opportunities to wit:
Direct the officer-in-charge of organizing the DVs to arrange in numerical sequence and attach the monthly report of checks issued to check the completeness of the DVs transmitted to COA and to always observe the highest standards of honesty, objectivity and consistency in the keeping of accounts to safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information; and
Attach a copy of documentary checklist in every DV as a guide on its proper supporting attachment. Also, direct all requesting personnel/department to ensure that all details on the supporting documents are completely and thoroughly filled up.

We recommended that the Management:
Hasten the preparation and submission of financial reports by availing the eNGAS and eBudget from the COA to facilitate systematic generation of financial reports;
Moreover, register from LBP we-access application to facilitate the prompt preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements, if warranted; and
Strictly adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

We recommended that the Management:
Ascertain and address the causes of the delay in the submission of transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.
Ensure that the required reports, disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other pertinent documents are completely submitted to the Accounting Office not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for the immediate recording in the books of accounts.
Further, give priority to the timely preparation and submission of the transaction documents for audit in such form and within the period prescribed by the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification/s on transacting with non PhilGEPS registered suppliers; and
Stop the practice of transacting with non PhilGEPS registered suppliers in order to comply with Sections 8.5.1 of the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management:
Revisit the provisions of the aforementioned joint circular on the grant of SRA and confirm with the DOH the payment of SRA to the qualified public health workers; and
Return the excess unutilized funds to DOH.

We recommended that the Management:
Thoroughly plan and implement the PPAs as scheduled so that the public can timely benefit the outcome; and
Direct the various departments within the Municipality (Accounting, Budget, Treasury, BAC, RHU, etc.) to properly coordinate with each other when there is a fund transfer in order to expedite its implementation and liquidation with the source agency.
